lesson_title: Past Experiences and Personal Stories: Reading Lesson 3
learning_objective: Learners can describe, compare and exchange simple information in a reading task about work.
cefr_outcome: Learners can communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring direct exchange of information.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise reading for past experiences and personal stories. You will learn useful language connected to work and practise countable/uncountable nouns. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at A2 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: countable/uncountable nouns. Vocabulary theme: work. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At A2, learners should describe, compare and exchange simple information with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use countable/uncountable nouns to talk about work.
Example 2: The learner gives a A2 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for A2.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using countable/uncountable nouns.
options: Correct use of countable/uncountable nouns in a A2 sentence.
Incorrect word order with countable/uncountable nouns.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses countable/uncountable nouns accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of countable/uncountable nouns.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the work phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand work.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand work.

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form countable/uncountable nouns. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about work using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: A2 Reading Check - Past Experiences and Personal Stories
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short reading task using countable/uncountable nouns and vocabulary from work.
success_criteria: Uses countable/uncountable nouns with reasonable accuracy
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses countable/uncountable nouns and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to work.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about work. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short A2 response about work. Include the target language: countable/uncountable nouns.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for A2-M02-L03. Speaker one: Today we are practising work. Speaker two: I need to use countable/uncountable nouns correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: Countable/Uncountable Nouns form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for countable/uncountable nouns
